decisionSummary: Keep temporary writable migration-candidate status. The fixture/temp-only watchdog migration proof is complete, but production high-risk gate enforcement is not changed because current write planning still uses the normal safe-write review path and live/production watchdog execution remains planned-disabled in source.

proofUsed.finding: review_write_plan approves allowlisted valid writes with backup and current-source checks; it has no high-risk watchdog requirement.
apply_setting_change_with_backup_manager creates a backup, calls review_write_plan, then applies the scalar write plan; no production screen-shader watchdog routing is present.
DryRunTempOnly watchdog execution is supported; ProductionPlannedDisabled, LiveConfigPlannedDisabled, and live config modes are planned but disabled by ensure_supported_watchdog_mode.

Fixture/temp-only screen-shader watchdog migration proof is complete, with plan-before-mutation, backup-before-mutation, separate-process confirm, timeout restore, result log, and display/render-independent recovery represented.
Tests prove the screen-shader watchdog migration flow against fixture/temp files only and record production enforcement unchanged.
Decision tests prove Option A fields, unchanged counts, unchanged allowlist, unchanged production enforcement, and continued normal write-plan approval in fixture review.

proofStillMissing: production write-flow primitive that requires a high-risk watchdog plan before applying decoration.screen_shader
test-backed production gate enforcement path that rejects normal path-only approval for decoration.screen_shader
explicit user-approved decision to change production enforcement behavior
standalone non-live shader compiler feasibility proof
nextRecommendedSprint: Screen shader production high-risk gate enforcement architecture sprint: design the production write-flow primitive that can require a watchdog plan before applying decoration.screen_shader, prove it with fixture/temp files only, keep compile-aware validation deferred, and do not run live shader compile or live display/render proof.
